preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Ssas trial user for an ai-backed design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is always eligible and opens every page. For a Mover at the conversion funnel stage, use the minimal variant — strip nav links, keep only the logo and a single 'Start Free Trial' CTA. This removes friction and keeps the persistent conversion action visible throughout the session, which is the highest-converting element on most SaaS pages.

sections.name: Hero — Single Action
sections.rationale: HeroPrimary supports Mover archetype and is effective at the conversion stage when configured as a decision-stage variant. The Mover variant suppresses the subhead and secondary CTA — one bold headline, one action. For a trial user already at conversion intent, this above-the-fold clarity eliminates deliberation and channels momentum directly into the trial signup.

sections.name: Silent Credibility Bar
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar supports Mover archetype and spans awareness through decision stages, reinforcing trust at the conversion moment. The Mover variant is logos-only with no headline — silent credibility that validates the product without interrupting forward motion. Placed directly below HeroPrimary, it answers 'who else uses this?' without slowing the Mover's pace toward trial signup.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics
sections.rationale: StatBlock supports Mover archetype at the consideration-to-decision stages, which directly feeds conversion confidence. The Mover variant suppresses sources and headlines — pure metric impact (e.g., '3× faster design handoff', '10K+ teams'). For a Mover at the conversion stage, unsourced but visually bold stats create momentum and reinforce the feeling that this is the obvious choice.

sections.name: Pricing — Trial Plan
sections.rationale: PricingCard supports Mover archetype at the decision and conversion funnel stages — this is the core conversion mechanism. The Mover variant strips descriptions down to price and CTA only, reducing cognitive load. Use the highlighted variant on the trial/recommended plan to create a single focal point. For a SaaS trial user at conversion, the pricing card is where intent becomes action.

sections.name: Final Conversion Banner
sections.rationale: CTABanner supports Mover archetype at decision and conversion funnel stages, serving as the terminal action prompt. The Mover variant uses a strong headline with a single CTA and no subhead — pure directional energy. Positioned after pricing, it recaptures any residual hesitation and provides one last decisive push toward trial signup for the Mover who scrolled past the earlier actions.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is always eligible and closes every page. Use the with-cta variant to recapture departing Mover users on this conversion-focused page. A final 'Start Free Trial' recovery CTA in the footer catches anyone who scrolled the entire page without converting, ensuring no Mover exits without one last opportunity to act.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Conversion funnel stage demands focused navigation with persistent CTA.
Mover variant suppresses subhead and cta_secondary — one message, one action. text-only aligns with Mover archetype's preference for minimal variants.

slots.headline: Design smarter. Ship faster. Start now.

page.reasoning: Mover variant is logos-only, no headline — silent credibility. Highest-frequency placement for Mover archetype in conversion contexts.
Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact. 3-stat is the preferred default and aligns with Mover's minimal approach.
highlighted variant for recommended plan to drive trial conversion. Mover variant strips description; price and CTA only — direct conversion focus.
Mover variant: strong headline, single CTA, no subhead. centered placement maximizes conversion impact at terminal action point.

slots.headline: Your trial is one click away.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Conversion stage warrants recovery CTA in footer.
